decisions.briefing: Markets are firmly risk-on today with NASDAQ up 1.5% and S&P 500 up 0.8%, a good backdrop for our tech-heavy portfolio. However, with only $180.54 in cash — well below the $500 threshold — we cannot open any new positions despite having 4 open slots. All six existing positions appear healthy with no stop-loss or profit-target triggers, so we hold everything and let the rally work in our favor.

market_education.summary_en: U.S. equities rallied broadly on April 24, 2026, led by technology and growth stocks as risk appetite returned to markets. The NASDAQ's outperformance (+1.5% vs S&P 500's +0.8%) signals that investors rotated into higher-beta, growth-oriented names, a pattern often seen following easing macro fears or positive earnings surprises from major tech companies. Semiconductor and AI-adjacent stocks dominated the top momentum movers, reflecting continued optimism around AI infrastructure spending cycles.
market_education.summary_zh: 2026年4月24日，美國股市全面上漲，科技與成長股領漲，市場風險偏好明顯回升。納斯達克指數漲幅（+1.5%）超越標普500（+0.8%），顯示投資者積極佈局高貝塔成長股，此現象通常出現在宏觀憂慮緩解或大型科技企業財報超預期之後。半導體及人工智能相關板塊主導動能榜單，反映市場對AI基礎建設支出週期持續樂觀。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: Cash drag is the performance penalty that occurs when a portfolio holds idle cash instead of invested assets. Today's risk-on rally is a perfect example: we have 4 open position slots and a favorable market, yet our $180.54 cash balance is too small to deploy, meaning we miss out on potential gains. Keeping cash reserves is important for flexibility, but too little deployable cash can mean missed opportunities when conditions are ideal.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「現金拖累」是指投資組合持有閒置現金而非投入資產所造成的績效損失。今天的風險偏好行情是最佳示例：我們有4個可用倉位、市場環境有利，但帳戶僅剩180.54美元現金，不足以建立新倉，因此錯失了潛在的上漲機會。保留現金儲備對靈活操作固然重要，但可部署的現金過少，往往意味著在最佳時機反而無法出手。
